name: Elsah Landing Cinnamon Rolls
recipeIngredient: 3 packages Active dry yeast (1/4 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
Combine milk, hot water, sugar (minus two teaspoons), salt, and shortening in a large mixing bowl. Stir until the shortening is melted.
Combine lukewarm water, yeast, and reserved two teaspoons of sugar in a small bowl. Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes or until the mixture expands and becomes bubbly.
Add eggs to the yeast mixture. Mix well. Gradually stir in flour until dough forms a ball that pulls away from the bowl. The dough will be soft.
Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8 to 10 minutes, or until smooth. Place the dough in a greased large bowl. Turn to grease the top. Cover with a clean dish towel and place in a warm, draft-free location. Let rise for 1 to 1 1/2 hours or until doubled.
Punch down the dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and roll into thin rectangles (approximately 1/4 inch thick).
Melt butter. Spread evenly over the rolled dough using a pastry brush. Combine brown sugar and cinnamon. Spread evenly on the buttered dough, reaching the edges.
Sprinkle raisins and nuts evenly over the dough surface.
Starting with the longest side, roll the dough into a log. Cut the log into slices 1 1/2 inches thick. Place the slices, cut side down, onto a greased baking pan measuring 12 x 18 inches. Let rise for about 1 hour, or until doubled.
Bake in a preheated oven at 425 degrees for 20 minutes, or until lightly browned.
Icing:
Combine sugar, vanilla, butter, and evaporated milk in a bowl. Beat with a mixer until smooth and creamy. Add more confectioner's sugar or milk if needed to achieve the desired spreading consistency.
